title: hieracium carpathicum subsp. subtumidum
intro: Hieracium carpathicum subsp. subtumidum is a rare subspecies of the Hieracium carpathicum plant, commonly known as the hawkweed, a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and skin conditions, such as eczema and acne. The health benefits of Hieracium carpathicum subsp. subtumidum include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe digestive problems and protect against cell damage. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by drying and storing the leaves for later use in teas, tinctures, or topical applications.
